shots.speech_text: The key measurement: Global mineral dust emission ~2,000 Tg/year; Saharan dust dominates at ~670 Tg/year; fluid threshold wind friction velocity for mineral dust ~0.20–0.25 m/s (Kok et al., 2021, Nature Reviews Earth & Environment). Results like this, produced by NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies (GISS), transformed our understanding of Aeolian dust lifting and atmospheric loading.
shots.visual_direction: EVIDENCE SHOT: [Data visualisation, telescope imagery, or archival footage of the key discovery moment]. Institution logos/credits if available. Measurement values as lower-third text overlay.

shots.speech_text: Aeolian dust lifting and atmospheric loading is not just a phenomenon at the far edge of science. It connects directly to questions about the fabric of reality. The e has implications that reach far beyond the laboratory.

shots.speech_text: We are only beginning to understand Aeolian dust lifting and atmospheric loading. And perhaps that's exactly the point — the universe keeps its most intricate secrets just within reach, if we keep looking.

guardrails: HELIX is voiceover only — no on-screen host, no synthetic presenter, no avatar.
All quantitative claims must include units and citation.
Uncertainty must be stated where present (e.g. 'approximately', 'within a margin of').
Do not present theoretical models as confirmed fact without qualification.
Do not fabricate measurements, paper titles, DOIs, or institutional affiliations.
Maintain wonder without hyperbole — the phenomena are extraordinary enough.
No speculation presented as established science.
HELIX does not cover history, biography, or language — those are DAVID's domain.
